title: Why You Should Avoid Cooking With These Oils

chunks.title: Introduction to Cooking Oils
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the various cooking oils available and their suitability for different cooking methods. They hint that many commonly used oils may not be healthy options.
chunks.transcript: cooking oils there's a huge selection of things that you can cook with whether you're cooking in a stainless steel pan or a cast iron pan what are you supposed to cook with whether you're cooking meat or you're cooking vegetables or you're cooking something else in a pan I'm going to go through all of that in this video and show you guys what I think are good cooking oils what I think are bad cooking oils and what I think are not so great cooking oils and I think you're going to be surprised because I bet you that a lot of the oils that you're cooking with or that you think are good are things that I am not going to

chunks.title: Pan Selection for Cooking
chunks.summary: Recommendations are made to use stainless steel or cast iron pans over non-stick options due to health concerns related to Teflon. The speaker describes a cooking technique to reduce sticking in stainless steel pans.
chunks.transcript: recommend so first things first let's start with your pan please cook in a stainless steel pan please do not use a non-stick pan for obvious reasons the predominant one being Teflon also known as PTFE which can aerosolize when you heat the pan can cause lung issues irritating for people with asthma or not if that PTFE flakes off you're going to end up eating it can potentially be harmful and endocrine disrupting so the pan that you're using is the first thing to think about use a stainless steel or a cast iron skillet and if you're worried about it sticking the stain inless steel pan there something called the Mercury ball effect if you just get the pan hot enough that you can throw some oil on the pan and it makes little little balls of water that run around the pan that kind of dance around the pan you know the pan is hot enough if you put oil on the pan when it's that hot you won't have many problems with sticking even on a stainless steel pan which is about the cleanest thing you

chunks.title: Dangers of Seed Oils
chunks.summary: The speaker expresses a strong aversion to seed oils, claiming they are toxic due to their processing and high susceptibility to oxidation. They argue that seed oils should not be part of the human diet at all.
chunks.transcript: can cook on some people worry about cast iron because they're getting more iron in their food I guess this depends on your Baseline iron status we've got seed oils on one end things like canola or margarine which is a lot of soybean oil got Olive avocado coconut you got some Tallow from lineage super proud of that and I've got some raw butter which you could also make into ghee so let's start with the seed oils as you guys know I'm a huge fan of seed oils this is something you should never even have in your home I feel bad even having it in my kitchen I use it as a prop but seed oils are a horrible thing to cook with they have tons of polyunsaturated fats which are fats that have multiple dou bonds this makes the fats very susceptible to oxidation when you heat them I don't even think you should use them as a salad dressing if don't heat them because they've been heated refined bleached and deodorized even before they come to you and they're sitting in this plastic bottle on the Shelf of a grocery store for for God knows how long I believe that seed oils because of this heavy industrial processing are basically toxic for humans from the moment they end up on a grocery shelf and have no place in the human diet humans have never consumed seed oils without consuming seeds or nuts in our history until the last 110 years this is a very recent addition to the human diet and though correlation is not causation there is a massive decline in our health over the last 110 years since things like this and Ultra processed foods which often contain them were introduced

chunks.title: Misconception About Smoke Point
chunks.summary: The speaker clarifies the misconception that a higher smoke point makes an oil safer for cooking, emphasizing the importance of considering the oil's peroxidation index instead. They discuss the stability of different fats.
chunks.transcript: into our diets previous to that they were used as engine lubricants and in World War II canola oil had a great role lubricating Parts on ships because canola oil apparently stays chunks.title: Evaluating Olive and Avocado Oils
chunks.summary: The potential drawbacks of olive and avocado oils are discussed, particularly their susceptibility to oxidation and the prevalence of adulteration with seed oils. The speaker concludes they are not ideal for cooking.

chunks.title: Benefits of Saturated Fats
chunks.summary: Saturated fats are declared healthy based on recent research, with the speaker advocating for animal fats like butter and tallow. They highlight the nutritional benefits these fats offer compared to plant fats.
chunks.transcript: cooking I know see right told you you learned something what do I think you should cook with the more saturated fats saturated fat has gotten a bad wrap for so long guys but if you chunks.title: Summary of Cooking Oils to Use
chunks.summary: The speaker concludes by summarizing the oils that are preferable for cooking, reiterating the dangers of widely used oils like canola and margarine, and emphasizing the benefits of coconut oil, butter, tallow, and ghee.
